creator: oleg@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Wed, Nov 28, 2007 at 08:10:02AM +0000, Denis V. Klimkov wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR kern/118128; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: "Denis V. Klimkov" <falcon@tcm.by>
> To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org, dyr@homelink.ru
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: kern/118128: [dummynet] Dummynet cause kernel trap or system freeze
> Date: Wed, 28 Nov 2007 09:44:15 +0200
> 
>  Hello bug-followup,
>  
>    I have almost the same problem but I get no kernel panic.
>    Just 100% of one of the CPUs is used by dummynet process and no
>    packet can go through network. I cannot ping even myself through
>    loopback. And I have to wait about 2-3 weeks before it happens. What
>    can I do next time it'll happen to get some debug information?
>  
>  -- 
>  Denis V. Klimkov
>  Network Administrator
>  TelecomMedia LLC
>  http://www.tcm.by
>  

First of all i need output of following commands:
uname -a
ipfw show
ipfw pipe show
ipfw queue show (if you are using queues).

-- 
Oleg.

================================================================
=== Oleg Bulyzhin -- OBUL-RIPN -- OBUL-RIPE -- oleg@rinet.ru ===
================================================================
